zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Clinton於1350年出生在英國瓦維其可爾馬可斯托可時，她的父親John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton11347歲，母親Idonea de Saye22歲。她於1374年之前與Baldwin de Montfort在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1375年在英國武斯特斯爾享年25歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Clinton于1350年出生在英国瓦維其可爾馬可斯托可时，她的父亲John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton11347岁，母亲Idonea de Saye22岁。她于1374年之前与Baldwin de Montfort在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1375年在英国武斯特斯爾享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Clinton was born in 1350, in Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England, her father, John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton, was 11347 and her mother, Idonea de Saye, was 22. She married Baldwin de Montfort before 1374, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1375, in Worcestershire, England, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Clinton est née en 1350, à Maxstoke, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton, avait 11347 ans et sa mère, Idonea de Saye, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Baldwin de Montfort avant 1374, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1375, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Clinton 1350 in Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton 11347 und ihre Mutter Idonea de Saye 22. Sie hat vor 1374 in England, United Kingdom Baldwin de Montfort geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1375 im Alter von 25 Jahren in Worcestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Clinton è nata il nel 1350 nel Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton, aveva 11347 anni e sua madre, Idonea de Saye, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Baldwin de Montfort prima del 1374, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1375, nel Worcestershire, England, all'età di 25 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Clinton(이)가 1350년 영국 워윅실르 맥스스토크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton(은)는 11347세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Idonea de Saye(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1374년 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Baldwin de Montfort(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1375년 영국 워스스터실르에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baldwin de Montfort перед 1374 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1375 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Clinton nació en 1350, en Maxstoke, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton, tenía 11347 años y su mamá, Idonea de Saye, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Baldwin de Montfort antes de 1374, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1375, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 25 años.
